themeWords.meaning: The proper name of the God of Israel
Judah; the southern kingdom/tribe, meaning praised

sentenceTheme: Jehoshaphat strengthens Judah, seeks the LORD, promotes teaching of the Law, and organizes a powerful military.

verses.text: And Jehoshaphat his son reigned in his stead, and strengthened himself against Israel.

highlightedWords.meaning: Jehovah has judged
Israel; he strives with God; the nation descended from Jacob

verses.text: And he placed forces in all the fenced cities of Judah, and set garrisons in the land of Judah, and in the cities of Ephraim, which Asa his father had taken.

highlightedWords.meaning: Judah; the southern kingdom/tribe, meaning praised
Ephraim; a son of Joseph, also the tribe/territory

verses.text: And the LORD was with Jehoshaphat, because he walked in the first ways of his father David, and sought not unto Baalim;

highlightedWords.meaning: The proper name of the God of Israel
Baal; a Canaanite deity (plural here: idols/baals)

verses.text: But sought to the Lord God of his father, and walked in his commandments, and not after the doings of Israel.

highlightedWords.meaning: God; the true God (also used of gods/judges)
Israel; he strives with God; the nation descended from Jacob

verses.text: Therefore the LORD stablished the kingdom in his hand; and all Judah brought to Jehoshaphat presents; and he had riches and honour in abundance.

highlightedWords.meaning: The proper name of the God of Israel
Judah; the southern kingdom/tribe, meaning praised

verses.text: And his heart was lifted up in the ways of the LORD: moreover he took away the high places and groves out of Judah.

highlightedWords.meaning: The proper name of the God of Israel
Asherah pole; a sacred tree or wooden object of idolatrous worship

verses.text: Also in the third year of his reign he sent to his princes, even to Ben-hail, and to Obadiah, and to Zechariah, and to Nethaneel, and to Michaiah, to teach in the cities of Judah.

highlightedWords.meaning: Prince, ruler, chief, captain, official
Judah; the southern kingdom/tribe, meaning praised

verses.text: And with them he sent Levites, even Shemaiah, and Nethaniah, and Zebadiah, and Asahel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehonathan, and Adonijah, and Tobijah, and Tobadonijah, Levites; and with them Elishama and Jehoram, priests.

highlightedWords.meaning: Levite; a member of the tribe of Levi
Priest; one who officiates in sacred rites

verses.text: And they taught in Judah, and had the book of the law of the LORD with them, and went about throughout all the cities of Judah, and taught the people.

highlightedWords.meaning: Law, instruction, teaching
The proper name of the God of Israel

verses.text: And the fear of the LORD fell upon all the kingdoms of the lands that were round about Judah, so that they made no war against Jehoshaphat.

highlightedWords.meaning: Dread, terror
The proper name of the God of Israel

verses.text: Also some of the Philistines brought Jehoshaphat presents, and tribute silver; and the Arabians brought him flocks, seven thousand and seven hundred rams, and seven thousand and seven hundred he goats.

highlightedWords.meaning: Philistines; inhabitants of Philistia
Arabians; people of Arabia

verses.text: And Jehoshaphat waxed great exceedingly; and he built in Judah castles, and cities of store.

highlightedWords.meaning: Jehovah has judged
Judah; the southern kingdom/tribe, meaning praised

verses.text: And he had much business in the cities of Judah: and the men of war, mighty men of valour, were in Jerusalem.

highlightedWords.meaning: Judah; the southern kingdom/tribe, meaning praised
Jerusalem; the chief city of Israel/Judah

verses.text: And these are the numbers of them according to the house of their fathers: Of Judah, the captains of thousands; Adnah the chief, and with him mighty men of valour three hundred thousand.

highlightedWords.meaning: Judah; the southern kingdom/tribe, meaning praised
A thousand (a numerical unit)

verses.text: And next to him was Jehohanan the captain, and with him two hundred and fourscore thousand.

highlightedWords.meaning: Prince, ruler, chief, captain
A thousand (a numerical unit)

verses.text: And next him was Amasiah the son of Zichri, who willingly offered himself unto the LORD; and with him two hundred thousand mighty men of valour.

highlightedWords.meaning: To volunteer, to offer freely
The proper name of the God of Israel

verses.text: And of Benjamin; Eliada a mighty man of valour, and with him armed men with bow and shield two hundred thousand.

highlightedWords.meaning: Benjamin; son of Jacob, also the tribe
Bow (as a weapon)

verses.text: And next him was Jehozabad, and with him an hundred and fourscore thousand ready prepared for the war.

highlightedWords.meaning: War, battle
A thousand (a numerical unit)

verses.text: These waited on the king, beside those whom the king put in the fenced cities throughout all Judah.

highlightedWords.meaning: King, ruler
Judah; the southern kingdom/tribe, meaning praised
